title: Shared fandom as undervalued social 'medicine' for division, isolation
excerpt: The state of the American social fabric—enormous, diverse and rent by stark partisan divides—is daunting enough that it can be hard to think about, let alone try to mend. At the Harvard Kennedy School, two social scientists are advancing the proposition that sports fandom, a powerful social phenomenon and force, may have a role to play in improving lives and strengthening societies.
content_html:
The state of the American social fabric—enormous, diverse and rent by stark partisan divides—is daunting enough that it can be hard to think about, let alone try to mend. At the Harvard Kennedy School, two social scientists are advancing the proposition that sports fandom, a powerful social phenomenon and force, may have a role to play in improving lives and strengthening societies.

That proposition is probably controversial. It's certainly underexplored.

So a new project, launched by professor Todd Rogers and doctoral student Audrey Feldman, aims to fill the gap, with its first wave of findings set to be published in the months ahead.

Leaving aside the billion-dollar business, fandom is an undeniable social fact. In the U.S., nearly three-quarters of people say they follow a pro sports team, and the majority of those consider their fandom to be an important part of their identity.

(And during the week of the World Cup final, it's noteworthy that 51% of people globally are fans of soccer alone. Americans are a bit less ardent (27%), but 40% planned to watch at least some of the games.)

Yet Rogers, Weatherhead professor of public policy at HKS, and Feldman have found that sports receive remarkably little scholarly attention. In top journals across the social sciences, only about 1 in 800 articles concerned sports.

The Fandom and Social Connection Initiative aims to change that.

Backed by a gift from FOX Sports and seeking partners among professional sports leagues, Rogers and Feldman have embraced the work as both an observational "green field" and a target for social intervention—with a personal rooting interest for them both.

Feldman was drawn to HKS in the hope that behavioral science could improve human lives—for instance, by increasing access to underutilized social programs and promoting learning and social cohesion in refugee populations.

But amid conversations with Rogers on international development, Feldman said, "we realized we're both big sports fans."

Feldman, who completed a bachelor's degree in economics at Notre Dame in 2024, didn't escape the pull of Fighting Irish football, though her dad went to rival Michigan. And Rogers's Mount Auburn Street office is dotted with Philadelphia Eagles gear.

The initiative attempts to apply rigorous modern tools to their shared passion.

Implicitly, the work involves a reclamation project for sports fandom itself, which Rogers argued is not only understudied but maligned.

"We did a survey where we asked nonfans to identify fans on that 'ascent of man' scale," Rogers said. Presented with that famous succession of increasingly hominid silhouettes ambling to the right, most nonfans placed passionate sports fans somewhere around Homo erectus.

To Rogers, that's a dangerously low appraisal of fandom's social role. "It's social infrastructure in a world where there are few—if any—institutions left that are this wide, and deep, and diverse," he said.

With many forms of social organization—social clubs, political parties and churches—in a kind of secular decline, sports fandoms loom larger than ever in their power to combine and motivate large groups of people across racial, economic and political divides.

Consider Congress. Nineteen of the 50 current state delegations are made up entirely of one party or the other.

By contrast, Rogers and Feldman found, the reddest NFL fan base (the Tennessee Titans) is still politically diverse, with two registered Republicans for every Democrat, while the bluest—the Los Angeles Chargers—is roughly the inverse.

And—as anyone knows who's ever watched a game in a packed stadium or bar—shared fandom can serve as an instant depolarizer.

In a paper currently under review, Rogers and Feldman applied game theory to test the effects.

In a computer survey, they presented participants with hypothetical "counterpartisans"—people who hold views on politics and culture starkly opposed to their own—and asked them to award a "bonus" to one or the other.

If they knew one "counterpartisan" also rooted for their favorite team, that one got the "bonus" at rates that outstripped those for counterpartisans with a shared race or ethnicity, education or home region.

Only a shared religion came close to being that influential, which seems revealing.

Often enough, sports fandom is as inherited as any other marker of identity. But—like religion—it's also both active and elective: a seasonal, ritual allegiance that people can either outgrow or choose to renew throughout their lives, Rogers argued.

And often enough, they do the latter—even when their favorite team is luckless, perennially "rebuilding" or, to all appearances, cursed.

To Rogers, that opting-in suggests that sports fandom isn't motivated principally by on-field events but by the bonds that form around them with parents, siblings, colleagues and neighbors. ("How else would there be Cleveland Browns fans?" he joked.)

Sports fandom—like religion—is both active and elective: a seasonal, ritual allegiance that people can either outgrow or choose to renew throughout their lives.

The positive social effects of being a sports fan have not entirely escaped serious notice before this summer. Rogers credits the book "Fans Have More Friends," co-written by Ben Valenta, head of strategy and analytics for FOX Sports. And he speaks monthly with Daniel Wann, professor at Kentucky's Murray State University, whom Rogers calls the underappreciated "godfather of psychology and fandom."

Wann gave him a useful metaphor. "He said shared fandom is like a shared photo album," Rogers recalled. To follow the same team as another person—even a perfect stranger—is "to know where [they] were in February 2018, and where they're going to be next September," he said. "Our hearts are going to be synchronized."

(Literally, Rogers added: Some psychophysiological results suggest that spectators' EEGs start to synchronize around the highs and lows that punctuate big games.)

Still near the outset of their shared work, Rogers and Feldman are theorizing why sports are such an effective social solvent—say, in conversations with a stranger. But it makes sense. Relative to the weather, they're engrossing, human and almost inexhaustible. And compared to politics or current events, sports are safe.

That's usually true even in cases of acrimonious rivalry, they argued. Feldman spoke of the fun of watching a football game even with fans of Ohio State. "I think their team is the worst, but at least we can talk about football," she said. "There's a mutual respect there."

Rogers noted one draft finding that backs that up: Fans of rival teams in the English Premier League said they'd rather socialize across the aisle separating, for example, Man United and Liverpool than with people who don't watch soccer at all.

Even as their theoretical work is ongoing, Rogers and Feldman are already pursuing opportunities to intervene in the real world.

Their first published work together was an op-ed published last fall for TIME, arguing that the football-averse should embrace a team in America's most popular sport. NFL fandom, they write, is a "simple and universally accessible medicine" in an age of loneliness.

That work will continue this summer, in partnership with the Boston Red Sox's Triple-A affiliate, looking for ways to nudge spectators into conversation with their neighbors in the bleachers.

Though their papers are still being written up, the first indications support the idea that sports can serve as a kind of social glue, Rogers said.

"Audrey and I have experiments where we can induce 40% of participants to watch a game with others, and—at the end—they feel more connected, and in a way that persists for weeks," he added.

Who's behind this story?

Sadie Harley

BSc Life Sciences & Ecology. Microbiology lab background with pharmaceutical news experience in oil, gas, and renewable industries. Full profile →

Andrew Zinin

Master's in physics with research experience. Long-time science news enthusiast. Plays key role in Science X's editorial success. Full profile →

This story is published courtesy of the Harvard Gazette, Harvard University's official newspaper. For additional university news, visit Harvard.edu.

Citation: Shared fandom as undervalued social 'medicine' for division, isolation (2026, July 19) retrieved 20 July 2026 from https://phys.org/news/2026-07-fandom-undervalued-social-medicine-division.html

blocks.html_en: The state of the American social fabric—enormous, diverse and rent by stark partisan divides—is daunting enough that it can be hard to think about, let alone try to mend. At the Harvard Kennedy School, two social scientists are advancing the proposition that sports fandom, a powerful social phenomenon and force, may have a role to play in improving lives and strengthening societies.

sentences.en: The state of the American social fabric—enormous, diverse and rent by stark partisan divides—is daunting enough that it can be hard to think about, let alone try to mend.
sentences.zh: 美国社会结构的现状——庞大、多样化，并且因严重的党派分歧而撕裂——已经足够令人畏惧，以至于很难思考，更不用说尝试修复了。

sentences.en: At the Harvard Kennedy School, two social scientists are advancing the proposition that sports fandom, a powerful social phenomenon and force, may have a role to play in improving lives and strengthening societies.
sentences.zh: 在哈佛大学肯尼迪学院，两位社会科学家正在提出这样的主张：体育迷是一种强大的社会现象和力量，可能在改善生活和加强社会方面发挥作用。

blocks.html_en: That proposition is probably controversial. It's certainly underexplored.

sentences.en: That proposition is probably controversial.
sentences.zh: 这个提议可能是有争议的。

sentences.en: It's certainly underexplored.
sentences.zh: 这肯定是尚未充分探索的。

blocks.html_en: So a new project, launched by professor Todd Rogers and doctoral student Audrey Feldman, aims to fill the gap, with its first wave of findings set to be published in the months ahead.

sentences.en: So a new project, launched by professor Todd Rogers and doctoral student Audrey Feldman, aims to fill the gap, with its first wave of findings set to be published in the months ahead.
sentences.zh: 因此，由托德·罗杰斯教授和博士生奥黛丽·费尔德曼发起的一个新项目旨在填补这一空白，其第一波研究结果将于未来几个月发布。

blocks.html_en: Leaving aside the billion-dollar business, fandom is an undeniable social fact. In the U.S., nearly three-quarters of people say they follow a pro sports team, and the majority of those consider their fandom to be an important part of their identity.

sentences.en: Leaving aside the billion-dollar business, fandom is an undeniable social fact.
sentences.zh: 撇开数十亿美元的生意不谈，粉丝群体是一个不可否认的社会事实。

sentences.en: In the U.S., nearly three-quarters of people say they follow a pro sports team, and the majority of those consider their fandom to be an important part of their identity.
sentences.zh: 在美国，近四分之三的人表示他们关注职业运动队，其中大多数人认为他们的粉丝是他们身份的重要组成部分。

blocks.html_en: (And during the week of the World Cup final, it's noteworthy that 51% of people globally are fans of soccer alone. Americans are a bit less ardent (27%), but 40% planned to watch at least some of the games.)

sentences.en: (And during the week of the World Cup final, it's noteworthy that 51% of people globally are fans of soccer alone.
sentences.zh: （值得注意的是，在世界杯决赛周期间，全球 51% 的人仅是足球迷。

sentences.en: Americans are a bit less ardent (27%), but 40% planned to watch at least some of the games.)
sentences.zh: 美国人的热情稍低一些（27%），但 40% 的人计划至少观看部分比赛。）

blocks.html_en: Yet Rogers, Weatherhead professor of public policy at HKS, and Feldman have found that sports receive remarkably little scholarly attention. In top journals across the social sciences, only about 1 in 800 articles concerned sports.

sentences.en: Yet Rogers, Weatherhead professor of public policy at HKS, and Feldman have found that sports receive remarkably little scholarly attention.
sentences.zh: 然而，HKS 公共政策教授罗杰斯和费尔德曼却发现，体育运动受到的学术关注非常少。

sentences.en: In top journals across the social sciences, only about 1 in 800 articles concerned sports.
sentences.zh: 在社会科学领域的顶级期刊中，只有大约八百篇文章中就有一篇涉及体育。

blocks.html_en: The Fandom and Social Connection Initiative aims to change that.

sentences.en: The Fandom and Social Connection Initiative aims to change that.
sentences.zh: 粉丝圈和社交联系计划旨在改变这一现状。

blocks.html_en: Backed by a gift from FOX Sports and seeking partners among professional sports leagues, Rogers and Feldman have embraced the work as both an observational "green field" and a target for social intervention—with a personal rooting interest for them both.

sentences.en: Backed by a gift from FOX Sports and seeking partners among professional sports leagues, Rogers and Feldman have embraced the work as both an observational "green field" and a target for social intervention—with a personal rooting interest for them both.
sentences.zh: 在福克斯体育频道的捐赠和在职业体育联盟中寻找合作伙伴的支持下，罗杰斯和费尔德曼将这项工作视为观察的“绿地”和社会干预的目标——他们俩都对这项工作抱有浓厚的兴趣。

blocks.html_en: Feldman was drawn to HKS in the hope that behavioral science could improve human lives—for instance, by increasing access to underutilized social programs and promoting learning and social cohesion in refugee populations.

sentences.en: Feldman was drawn to HKS in the hope that behavioral science could improve human lives—for instance, by increasing access to underutilized social programs and promoting learning and social cohesion in refugee populations.
sentences.zh: 费尔德曼被 HKS 所吸引，是希望行为科学能够改善人类生活，例如，通过增加未充分利用的社会项目的机会以及促进难民群体的学习和社会凝聚力。

blocks.html_en: But amid conversations with Rogers on international development, Feldman said, "we realized we're both big sports fans."

sentences.en: But amid conversations with Rogers on international development, Feldman said, "we realized we're both big sports fans."
sentences.zh: 但在与罗杰斯讨论国际发展问题时，费尔德曼说，“我们意识到我们都是体育迷。”

blocks.html_en: Feldman, who completed a bachelor's degree in economics at Notre Dame in 2024, didn't escape the pull of Fighting Irish football, though her dad went to rival Michigan. And Rogers's Mount Auburn Street office is dotted with Philadelphia Eagles gear.

sentences.en: Feldman, who completed a bachelor's degree in economics at Notre Dame in 2024, didn't escape the pull of Fighting Irish football, though her dad went to rival Michigan.
sentences.zh: 费尔德曼于 2024 年在圣母大学获得了经济学学士学位，尽管她的父亲去了对手密歇根大学，但她并没有逃脱“战斗爱尔兰足球”的影响。

sentences.en: And Rogers's Mount Auburn Street office is dotted with Philadelphia Eagles gear.
sentences.zh: 罗杰斯位于奥本山街的办公室里散布着费城老鹰队的装备。

blocks.html_en: The initiative attempts to apply rigorous modern tools to their shared passion.

sentences.en: The initiative attempts to apply rigorous modern tools to their shared passion.
sentences.zh: 该倡议试图将严格的现代工具应用于他们共同的热情。

blocks.html_en: Implicitly, the work involves a reclamation project for sports fandom itself, which Rogers argued is not only understudied but maligned.

sentences.en: Implicitly, the work involves a reclamation project for sports fandom itself, which Rogers argued is not only understudied but maligned.
sentences.zh: 隐含地，这项工作涉及体育迷本身的一项回收项目，罗杰斯认为该项目不仅没有得到充分研究，而且受到了诽谤。

blocks.html_en: "We did a survey where we asked nonfans to identify fans on that 'ascent of man' scale," Rogers said. Presented with that famous succession of increasingly hominid silhouettes ambling to the right, most nonfans placed passionate sports fans somewhere around Homo erectus.

sentences.en: "We did a survey where we asked nonfans to identify fans on that 'ascent of man' scale," Rogers said.
sentences.zh: 罗杰斯说：“我们做了一项调查，要求非粉丝根据‘人类进步’等级来识别粉丝。”

sentences.en: Presented with that famous succession of increasingly hominid silhouettes ambling to the right, most nonfans placed passionate sports fans somewhere around Homo erectus.
sentences.zh: 当看到一系列著名的、越来越多的原始人剪影向右漫步时，大多数非球迷将热情的体育迷放在直立人周围的某个地方。

blocks.html_en: To Rogers, that's a dangerously low appraisal of fandom's social role. "It's social infrastructure in a world where there are few—if any—institutions left that are this wide, and deep, and diverse," he said.

sentences.en: To Rogers, that's a dangerously low appraisal of fandom's social role.
sentences.zh: 对于罗杰斯来说，这是对粉丝社会角色的危险的低评价。

sentences.en: "It's social infrastructure in a world where there are few—if any—institutions left that are this wide, and deep, and diverse," he said.
sentences.zh: “这是社会基础设施，在这个世界上，如此广泛、如此深入、如此多样化的机构已经很少了——如果有的话，”他说。

blocks.html_en: With many forms of social organization—social clubs, political parties and churches—in a kind of secular decline, sports fandoms loom larger than ever in their power to combine and motivate large groups of people across racial, economic and political divides.

sentences.en: With many forms of social organization—social clubs, political parties and churches—in a kind of secular decline, sports fandoms loom larger than ever in their power to combine and motivate large groups of people across racial, economic and political divides.
sentences.zh: 随着多种形式的社会组织（社交俱乐部、政党和教会）的长期衰落，体育迷群体在团结和激励跨越种族、经济和政治分歧的大批人群方面的力量比以往任何时候都更加强大。

blocks.html_en: Consider Congress. Nineteen of the 50 current state delegations are made up entirely of one party or the other.

sentences.en: Consider Congress.
sentences.zh: 考虑一下国会。

sentences.en: Nineteen of the 50 current state delegations are made up entirely of one party or the other.
sentences.zh: 现有 50 个国家代表团中有 19 个完全由一方或另一方组成。

blocks.html_en: By contrast, Rogers and Feldman found, the reddest NFL fan base (the Tennessee Titans) is still politically diverse, with two registered Republicans for every Democrat, while the bluest—the Los Angeles Chargers—is roughly the inverse.

sentences.en: By contrast, Rogers and Feldman found, the reddest NFL fan base (the Tennessee Titans) is still politically diverse, with two registered Republicans for every Democrat, while the bluest—the Los Angeles Chargers—is roughly the inverse.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，罗杰斯和费尔德曼发现，最红的 NFL 球迷群体（田纳西泰坦队）在政治上仍然是多元化的，登记的共和党人与民主党人的比例为 2，而最蓝的洛杉矶闪电队的情况则大致相反。

blocks.html_en: And—as anyone knows who's ever watched a game in a packed stadium or bar—shared fandom can serve as an instant depolarizer.

sentences.en: And—as anyone knows who's ever watched a game in a packed stadium or bar—shared fandom can serve as an instant depolarizer.
sentences.zh: 而且，凡是在拥挤的体育场或酒吧观看过比赛的人都知道，共同的球迷群体可以立即消除两极分化。

blocks.html_en: In a paper currently under review, Rogers and Feldman applied game theory to test the effects.

sentences.en: In a paper currently under review, Rogers and Feldman applied game theory to test the effects.
sentences.zh: 在目前正在审查的一篇论文中，罗杰斯和费尔德曼应用博弈论来测试效果。

blocks.html_en: In a computer survey, they presented participants with hypothetical "counterpartisans"—people who hold views on politics and culture starkly opposed to their own—and asked them to award a "bonus" to one or the other.

sentences.en: In a computer survey, they presented participants with hypothetical "counterpartisans"—people who hold views on politics and culture starkly opposed to their own—and asked them to award a "bonus" to one or the other.
sentences.zh: 在一项计算机调查中，他们向参与者展示了假设的“对手党派”——在政治和文化上持有与自己截然相反的观点的人——并要求他们向其中一个或另一个颁发“奖金”。

blocks.html_en: If they knew one "counterpartisan" also rooted for their favorite team, that one got the "bonus" at rates that outstripped those for counterpartisans with a shared race or ethnicity, education or home region.

sentences.en: If they knew one "counterpartisan" also rooted for their favorite team, that one got the "bonus" at rates that outstripped those for counterpartisans with a shared race or ethnicity, education or home region.
sentences.zh: 如果他们知道一名“对手”也支持他们最喜欢的球队，那么该人获得“奖金”的比例就会超过具有共同种族或民族、教育或家乡地区的对手。

blocks.html_en: Only a shared religion came close to being that influential, which seems revealing.

sentences.en: Only a shared religion came close to being that influential, which seems revealing.
sentences.zh: 只有共同的宗教才有如此大的影响力，这似乎很能说明问题。

blocks.html_en: Often enough, sports fandom is as inherited as any other marker of identity. But—like religion—it's also both active and elective: a seasonal, ritual allegiance that people can either outgrow or choose to renew throughout their lives, Rogers argued.

sentences.en: Often enough, sports fandom is as inherited as any other marker of identity.
sentences.zh: 通常，体育迷和任何其他身份标志一样都是继承性的。

sentences.en: But—like religion—it's also both active and elective: a seasonal, ritual allegiance that people can either outgrow or choose to renew throughout their lives, Rogers argued.
sentences.zh: 但罗杰斯认为，就像宗教一样，它也是主动的和选择性的：一种季节性的、仪式性的忠诚，人们可以在一生中摆脱这种忠诚，也可以选择在一生中更新。

blocks.html_en: And often enough, they do the latter—even when their favorite team is luckless, perennially "rebuilding" or, to all appearances, cursed.

sentences.en: And often enough, they do the latter—even when their favorite team is luckless, perennially "rebuilding" or, to all appearances, cursed.
sentences.zh: 通常，他们会选择后者——即使他们最喜欢的球队运气不佳，一直在“重建”，或者从各方面来看，都受到了诅咒。

blocks.html_en: To Rogers, that opting-in suggests that sports fandom isn't motivated principally by on-field events but by the bonds that form around them with parents, siblings, colleagues and neighbors. ("How else would there be Cleveland Browns fans?" he joked.)

sentences.en: To Rogers, that opting-in suggests that sports fandom isn't motivated principally by on-field events but by the bonds that form around them with parents, siblings, colleagues and neighbors.
sentences.zh: 对罗杰斯来说，这种选择表明体育迷的动机主要不是球场上的赛事，而是他们与父母、兄弟姐妹、同事和邻居之间形成的纽带。

sentences.en: ("How else would there be Cleveland Browns fans?"
sentences.zh: （“不然怎么会有克利夫兰布朗队的球迷呢？”

sentences.en: he joked.)
sentences.zh: 他开玩笑说。）

blocks.html_en: Sports fandom—like religion—is both active and elective: a seasonal, ritual allegiance that people can either outgrow or choose to renew throughout their lives.

sentences.en: Sports fandom—like religion—is both active and elective: a seasonal, ritual allegiance that people can either outgrow or choose to renew throughout their lives.
sentences.zh: 体育迷就像宗教一样，既是积极的又是选择性的：一种季节性的、仪式性的忠诚，人们可以在一生中逐渐摆脱这种忠诚，也可以选择更新。

blocks.html_en: The positive social effects of being a sports fan have not entirely escaped serious notice before this summer. Rogers credits the book "Fans Have More Friends," co-written by Ben Valenta, head of strategy and analytics for FOX Sports. And he speaks monthly with Daniel Wann, professor at Kentucky's Murray State University, whom Rogers calls the underappreciated "godfather of psychology and fandom."

sentences.en: The positive social effects of being a sports fan have not entirely escaped serious notice before this summer.
sentences.zh: 在今年夏天之前，体育迷所带来的积极社会影响并未完全被忽视。

sentences.en: Rogers credits the book "Fans Have More Friends," co-written by Ben Valenta, head of strategy and analytics for FOX Sports.
sentences.zh: 罗杰斯将此归功于福克斯体育频道战略与分析主管本·瓦伦塔 (Ben Valenta) 与人合着的《球迷有更多朋友》一书。

sentences.en: And he speaks monthly with Daniel Wann, professor at Kentucky's Murray State University, whom Rogers calls the underappreciated "godfather of psychology and fandom."
sentences.zh: 他每月都会与肯塔基州默里州立大学教授丹尼尔·万恩进行交谈，罗杰斯称他为未被充分赏识的“心理学和粉丝教父”。

blocks.html_en: Wann gave him a useful metaphor. "He said shared fandom is like a shared photo album," Rogers recalled. To follow the same team as another person—even a perfect stranger—is "to know where [they] were in February 2018, and where they're going to be next September," he said. "Our hearts are going to be synchronized."

sentences.en: Wann gave him a useful metaphor.
sentences.zh: 万恩给了他一个有用的比喻。

sentences.en: "He said shared fandom is like a shared photo album," Rogers recalled.
sentences.zh: “他说共享的粉丝就像共享的相册，”罗杰斯回忆道。

sentences.en: To follow the same team as another person—even a perfect stranger—is "to know where [they] were in February 2018, and where they're going to be next September," he said.
sentences.zh: 他说，与另一个人（即使是完全陌生的人）一起关注同一个团队就是“知道他们在 2018 年 2 月在哪里，以及明年 9 月他们将在哪里”。

sentences.en: "Our hearts are going to be synchronized."
sentences.zh: “我们的心将会同步。”

blocks.html_en: (Literally, Rogers added: Some psychophysiological results suggest that spectators' EEGs start to synchronize around the highs and lows that punctuate big games.)

sentences.en: (Literally, Rogers added: Some psychophysiological results suggest that spectators' EEGs start to synchronize around the highs and lows that punctuate big games.)
sentences.zh: （从字面上看，罗杰斯补充道：一些心理生理学结果表明，观众的脑电图开始与大型比赛中的高点和低点同步。）

blocks.html_en: Still near the outset of their shared work, Rogers and Feldman are theorizing why sports are such an effective social solvent—say, in conversations with a stranger. But it makes sense. Relative to the weather, they're engrossing, human and almost inexhaustible. And compared to politics or current events, sports are safe.

sentences.en: Still near the outset of their shared work, Rogers and Feldman are theorizing why sports are such an effective social solvent—say, in conversations with a stranger.
sentences.zh: 罗杰斯和费尔德曼的共同工作刚刚开始，他们就在理论上解释为什么体育运动是一种如此有效的社会溶剂——比如，在与陌生人的对话中。

sentences.en: But it makes sense.
sentences.zh: 但这是有道理的。

sentences.en: Relative to the weather, they're engrossing, human and almost inexhaustible.
sentences.zh: 相对于天气来说，它们是引人入胜的、人性化的，而且几乎取之不尽用之不竭。

sentences.en: And compared to politics or current events, sports are safe.
sentences.zh: 与政治或时事相比，体育是安全的。

blocks.html_en: That's usually true even in cases of acrimonious rivalry, they argued. Feldman spoke of the fun of watching a football game even with fans of Ohio State. "I think their team is the worst, but at least we can talk about football," she said. "There's a mutual respect there."

sentences.en: That's usually true even in cases of acrimonious rivalry, they argued.
sentences.zh: 他们认为，即使在激烈的竞争中也是如此。

sentences.en: Feldman spoke of the fun of watching a football game even with fans of Ohio State.
sentences.zh: 费尔德曼谈到了与俄亥俄州立大学球迷一起观看足球比赛的乐趣。

sentences.en: "I think their team is the worst, but at least we can talk about football," she said.
sentences.zh: “我认为他们的球队是最差的，但至少我们可以谈论足球，”她说。

sentences.en: "There's a mutual respect there."
sentences.zh: “那里有相互尊重。”

blocks.html_en: Rogers noted one draft finding that backs that up: Fans of rival teams in the English Premier League said they'd rather socialize across the aisle separating, for example, Man United and Liverpool than with people who don't watch soccer at all.

sentences.en: Rogers noted one draft finding that backs that up: Fans of rival teams in the English Premier League said they'd rather socialize across the aisle separating, for example, Man United and Liverpool than with people who don't watch soccer at all.
sentences.zh: 罗杰斯指出，一项选秀调查结果支持了这一点：英超联赛中对手球队的球迷表示，他们宁愿在分开的过道上进行社交活动，例如曼联和利物浦，也不愿与根本不看足球的人进行社交活动。

blocks.html_en: Even as their theoretical work is ongoing, Rogers and Feldman are already pursuing opportunities to intervene in the real world.

sentences.en: Even as their theoretical work is ongoing, Rogers and Feldman are already pursuing opportunities to intervene in the real world.
sentences.zh: 尽管他们的理论工作正在进行中，罗杰斯和费尔德曼已经在寻找干预现实世界的机会。

blocks.html_en: Their first published work together was an op-ed published last fall for TIME, arguing that the football-averse should embrace a team in America's most popular sport. NFL fandom, they write, is a "simple and universally accessible medicine" in an age of loneliness.

sentences.en: Their first published work together was an op-ed published last fall for TIME, arguing that the football-averse should embrace a team in America's most popular sport.
sentences.zh: 他们共同发表的第一篇作品是去年秋天在《时代》杂志上发表的一篇专栏文章，认为厌恶橄榄球的人应该拥抱美国最受欢迎的运动的球队。

sentences.en: NFL fandom, they write, is a "simple and universally accessible medicine" in an age of loneliness.
sentences.zh: 他们写道，NFL 球迷群体是孤独时代的一剂“简单且普遍可用的良药”。

blocks.html_en: That work will continue this summer, in partnership with the Boston Red Sox's Triple-A affiliate, looking for ways to nudge spectators into conversation with their neighbors in the bleachers.

sentences.en: That work will continue this summer, in partnership with the Boston Red Sox's Triple-A affiliate, looking for ways to nudge spectators into conversation with their neighbors in the bleachers.
sentences.zh: 这项工作将于今年夏天继续与波士顿红袜队的 3A 附属机构合作，寻找方法促使观众与露天看台上的邻居进行对话。

blocks.html_en: Though their papers are still being written up, the first indications support the idea that sports can serve as a kind of social glue, Rogers said.

sentences.en: Though their papers are still being written up, the first indications support the idea that sports can serve as a kind of social glue, Rogers said.
sentences.zh: 罗杰斯说，尽管他们的论文仍在撰写中，但初步迹象支持了体育可以作为一种社会粘合剂的想法。

blocks.html_en: "Audrey and I have experiments where we can induce 40% of participants to watch a game with others, and—at the end—they feel more connected, and in a way that persists for weeks," he added.

sentences.en: "Audrey and I have experiments where we can induce 40% of participants to watch a game with others, and—at the end—they feel more connected, and in a way that persists for weeks," he added.
sentences.zh: “奥黛丽和我进行了实验，我们可以诱导 40% 的参与者与其他人一起观看比赛，最后，他们会感觉联系更加紧密，而且这种联系会持续数周，”他补充道。

content_html_zh:
美国社会结构的现状——庞大、多样化，并且因严重的党派分歧而撕裂——已经足够令人畏惧，以至于很难思考，更不用说尝试修复了。 在哈佛大学肯尼迪学院，两位社会科学家正在提出这样的主张：体育迷是一种强大的社会现象和力量，可能在改善生活和加强社会方面发挥作用。

这个提议可能是有争议的。 这肯定是尚未充分探索的。

因此，由托德·罗杰斯教授和博士生奥黛丽·费尔德曼发起的一个新项目旨在填补这一空白，其第一波研究结果将于未来几个月发布。

撇开数十亿美元的生意不谈，粉丝群体是一个不可否认的社会事实。 在美国，近四分之三的人表示他们关注职业运动队，其中大多数人认为他们的粉丝是他们身份的重要组成部分。

（值得注意的是，在世界杯决赛周期间，全球 51% 的人仅是足球迷。 美国人的热情稍低一些（27%），但 40% 的人计划至少观看部分比赛。）

然而，HKS 公共政策教授罗杰斯和费尔德曼却发现，体育运动受到的学术关注非常少。 在社会科学领域的顶级期刊中，只有大约八百篇文章中就有一篇涉及体育。

粉丝圈和社交联系计划旨在改变这一现状。

在福克斯体育频道的捐赠和在职业体育联盟中寻找合作伙伴的支持下，罗杰斯和费尔德曼将这项工作视为观察的“绿地”和社会干预的目标——他们俩都对这项工作抱有浓厚的兴趣。

费尔德曼被 HKS 所吸引，是希望行为科学能够改善人类生活，例如，通过增加未充分利用的社会项目的机会以及促进难民群体的学习和社会凝聚力。

但在与罗杰斯讨论国际发展问题时，费尔德曼说，“我们意识到我们都是体育迷。”

费尔德曼于 2024 年在圣母大学获得了经济学学士学位，尽管她的父亲去了对手密歇根大学，但她并没有逃脱“战斗爱尔兰足球”的影响。 罗杰斯位于奥本山街的办公室里散布着费城老鹰队的装备。

该倡议试图将严格的现代工具应用于他们共同的热情。

隐含地，这项工作涉及体育迷本身的一项回收项目，罗杰斯认为该项目不仅没有得到充分研究，而且受到了诽谤。

罗杰斯说：“我们做了一项调查，要求非粉丝根据‘人类进步’等级来识别粉丝。” 当看到一系列著名的、越来越多的原始人剪影向右漫步时，大多数非球迷将热情的体育迷放在直立人周围的某个地方。

对于罗杰斯来说，这是对粉丝社会角色的危险的低评价。 “这是社会基础设施，在这个世界上，如此广泛、如此深入、如此多样化的机构已经很少了——如果有的话，”他说。

随着多种形式的社会组织（社交俱乐部、政党和教会）的长期衰落，体育迷群体在团结和激励跨越种族、经济和政治分歧的大批人群方面的力量比以往任何时候都更加强大。

考虑一下国会。 现有 50 个国家代表团中有 19 个完全由一方或另一方组成。

相比之下，罗杰斯和费尔德曼发现，最红的 NFL 球迷群体（田纳西泰坦队）在政治上仍然是多元化的，登记的共和党人与民主党人的比例为 2，而最蓝的洛杉矶闪电队的情况则大致相反。

而且，凡是在拥挤的体育场或酒吧观看过比赛的人都知道，共同的球迷群体可以立即消除两极分化。

在目前正在审查的一篇论文中，罗杰斯和费尔德曼应用博弈论来测试效果。

在一项计算机调查中，他们向参与者展示了假设的“对手党派”——在政治和文化上持有与自己截然相反的观点的人——并要求他们向其中一个或另一个颁发“奖金”。

如果他们知道一名“对手”也支持他们最喜欢的球队，那么该人获得“奖金”的比例就会超过具有共同种族或民族、教育或家乡地区的对手。

只有共同的宗教才有如此大的影响力，这似乎很能说明问题。

通常，体育迷和任何其他身份标志一样都是继承性的。 但罗杰斯认为，就像宗教一样，它也是主动的和选择性的：一种季节性的、仪式性的忠诚，人们可以在一生中摆脱这种忠诚，也可以选择在一生中更新。

通常，他们会选择后者——即使他们最喜欢的球队运气不佳，一直在“重建”，或者从各方面来看，都受到了诅咒。

对罗杰斯来说，这种选择表明体育迷的动机主要不是球场上的赛事，而是他们与父母、兄弟姐妹、同事和邻居之间形成的纽带。 （“不然怎么会有克利夫兰布朗队的球迷呢？” 他开玩笑说。）

体育迷就像宗教一样，既是积极的又是选择性的：一种季节性的、仪式性的忠诚，人们可以在一生中逐渐摆脱这种忠诚，也可以选择更新。

在今年夏天之前，体育迷所带来的积极社会影响并未完全被忽视。 罗杰斯将此归功于福克斯体育频道战略与分析主管本·瓦伦塔 (Ben Valenta) 与人合着的《球迷有更多朋友》一书。 他每月都会与肯塔基州默里州立大学教授丹尼尔·万恩进行交谈，罗杰斯称他为未被充分赏识的“心理学和粉丝教父”。

万恩给了他一个有用的比喻。 “他说共享的粉丝就像共享的相册，”罗杰斯回忆道。 他说，与另一个人（即使是完全陌生的人）一起关注同一个团队就是“知道他们在 2018 年 2 月在哪里，以及明年 9 月他们将在哪里”。 “我们的心将会同步。”

（从字面上看，罗杰斯补充道：一些心理生理学结果表明，观众的脑电图开始与大型比赛中的高点和低点同步。）

罗杰斯和费尔德曼的共同工作刚刚开始，他们就在理论上解释为什么体育运动是一种如此有效的社会溶剂——比如，在与陌生人的对话中。 但这是有道理的。 相对于天气来说，它们是引人入胜的、人性化的，而且几乎取之不尽用之不竭。 与政治或时事相比，体育是安全的。

他们认为，即使在激烈的竞争中也是如此。 费尔德曼谈到了与俄亥俄州立大学球迷一起观看足球比赛的乐趣。 “我认为他们的球队是最差的，但至少我们可以谈论足球，”她说。 “那里有相互尊重。”

罗杰斯指出，一项选秀调查结果支持了这一点：英超联赛中对手球队的球迷表示，他们宁愿在分开的过道上进行社交活动，例如曼联和利物浦，也不愿与根本不看足球的人进行社交活动。

尽管他们的理论工作正在进行中，罗杰斯和费尔德曼已经在寻找干预现实世界的机会。

他们共同发表的第一篇作品是去年秋天在《时代》杂志上发表的一篇专栏文章，认为厌恶橄榄球的人应该拥抱美国最受欢迎的运动的球队。 他们写道，NFL 球迷群体是孤独时代的一剂“简单且普遍可用的良药”。

这项工作将于今年夏天继续与波士顿红袜队的 3A 附属机构合作，寻找方法促使观众与露天看台上的邻居进行对话。

罗杰斯说，尽管他们的论文仍在撰写中，但初步迹象支持了体育可以作为一种社会粘合剂的想法。

“奥黛丽和我进行了实验，我们可以诱导 40% 的参与者与其他人一起观看比赛，最后，他们会感觉联系更加紧密，而且这种联系会持续数周，”他补充道。

谁是这个故事的幕后黑手？
